bullets: Conducted **interdisciplinary research** at the intersection of **AI, medical imaging, ultrasound systems, and image-guided cancer intervention**.

**Published 11 peer-reviewed research papers** at leading medical imaging journals and conferences, advancing state-of-the-art methods in prostate cancer imaging, elastography, and AI-driven intervention systems.

**Led** end-to-end clinical data acquisition and developed multi-modal analysis pipelines for the **OPTiMAL clinical trial** (BC Cancer & UBC), integrating MRI and ultrasound data from operating rooms and clinical environments for quantitative prostate cancer research.

Developed **three ultrasound elastography systems (3D + multi-frequency shear wave imaging)** by solving deep algorithmic and hardware bottlenecks, **unblocking projects stalled for 10+ years** and delivering publications in IEEE TMI, IEEE TUFFC, and IUS.

Designed and implemented **novel adversarial learning systems for automated prostate brachytherapy planning**, reducing plan generation time from **~20 minutes to ~3 seconds (~400x speedup)** (MICCAI, IPCAI).

Developed registration pipelines for transperineal template mapping biopsy, integrating multi-modal imaging (ultrasound, MRI) and multi-parametric data (anatomical, functional) to enable accurate targeting and treatment delivery.

**Extended capabilities of commercial micro-ultrasound systems (ExactVu)** by enabling shear wave and strain imaging on high-frequency probes, validating new clinical use cases for next-generation prostate imaging platforms.
thesis: **Prostate cancer detection with ultrasonic elastography and automated radiation treatment planning using Generative Adversarial Networks.**
supervisor: Dr. Tim Salcudean and Dr. Sara Mahdavi
scholarships: **Affiliated Doctoral Scholarship**, Faculty of Graduate and Postdoctoral Studies, University of British Columbia: This **highly competitive scholarship** is awarded to **highly meritorious** students following a university-wide competition.

**Assortment of Awards from UBC**, University of British Columbia: Received the Faculty of Applied Science Graduate Award, International Tuition Award, and President's Academic Excellence Initiative PhD Award, in recognition of academic achievements.

**Dr. Deepak Kaura Award in the Mathematics of Medicine**, Institute of Applied Mathematics Department, University of British Columbia: Awarded in recognition of **outstanding interdisciplinary work in leveraging mathematical tools to advance medical imaging.**

degree: MSc in Medical Imaging and Applications (ERASMUS+ Mundus)
institution: University of Burgundy, University of Cassino, University of Girona, and Radboud University Medical Center
bullets: **Highly competitive Erasmus Mundus program** across 3 European universities and research laboratories, with extremely limited global intake (**maximum ~2 students admitted per country annually**) and full funding through a **prestigious scholarship**.
**120-credit joint European MSc** in Medical Imaging and Applications combining advanced coursework, research, and international collaboration.
**Published 4 peer-reviewed research articles** within the 2-year MSc program.

Built **strong expertise in AI, computer vision, and medical imaging** through advanced graduate coursework in image analysis, pattern recognition, image segmentation, image registration, computer-aided diagnosis, medical robotics, distributed systems, and parallel computing.
Conducted extensive laboratory work, technical projects, and research activities involving medical imaging technologies, computational methods, and healthcare applications.

MSc thesis research was conducted at the **Radboud University Medical Center** in the Netherlands, a leading institution in medical imaging research, under the supervision of Dr. Colin Jacobs and Dr. Bram van Ginneken.
Collaborated and studied in **multicultural academic environments across Europe**, gaining strong cross-cultural communication, adaptability, and international research experience.
thesis: **Early detection of cancer in pulmonary nodules using Prior CT Lung scans.**
supervisor: Dr. Colin Jacobs and Dr. Bram van Ginneken
scholarships: **Erasmus Mundus Joint Master Scholarship (65,000 CAD)**, European Union: **Highly competitive & prestigious** scholarship offered to only a select few top-ranked applicants.

degree: BSc in Electrical and Electronic Engineering
bullets: Completed a **145-credit program across 59 courses**, covering electrical engineering, electronics, telecommunications, embedded systems, signal processing, and electromagnetics.
Graduated with a **CGPA of 3.96/4.00** and **Summa Cum Laude distinction**, the highest academic honor.
**Published 3 research articles** focused on antenna design and applied electromagnetics.

Built **strong engineering foundations through advanced coursework** in control systems, power systems, VLSI design, microprocessors, embedded systems, analog/digital electronics, and antenna and communication engineering.

Developed **extensive hands-on experience** through integrated laboratory training in electrical circuits, electronics, telecommunications, electrical machines, and hardware-focused engineering projects.

Gained strong analytical and computational problem-solving skills through advanced mathematics, signal processing, electromagnetic theory, Fourier analysis, Laplace transforms, statistics, and modern physics.
Conducted undergraduate **thesis research in biomedical antenna design** for healthcare applications
thesis: **Implantable miniaturized slot dipole patch antenna for in-body biomedical applications.**
scholarships: **Academic Scholarship**: Merit-based scholarship for **exceptional academic performance** throughout undergraduate studies.
**Summa Cum Laude Distinction**: **Highest academic honour** for completing BSc with a CGPA of 3.96/4.00 under a full course load.
